learning_outcomes.description: Describe the basic biology (diet, distribution, habitat requirements), evolution, taxonomy, and threats of and be able to identify Australian vertebrates
Describe vertebrate communities in Australian habitats
Collect, analyse and organize information and features from wildlife scats and skulls to accurate describe each one and identify the species it originated from.

Gain the ability to interact and communicate effectively with others in order to achieve fieldwork outcomes and the ability to apply the principles required to safely trap and handle Australian wildlife.
Understand how to use dichotomous keys to identify animals
Apply the principles of proper referencing and methods to avoid plagiarism.

assessments.due_date:
1) Scat ID practical report 21/08/2026 4:00 pm

2) Skull ID practical report 4/09/2026 4:00 pm

3) Traces ID practical report 11/09/2026 4:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
Scat ID practical report (10%)

This assessment is based on the practical on the identification of animals from their scats. Students will describe the characteristic features of each item and identify the species from which it came.

Skull ID practical report (10%)

This assessment item is based on the practical on the identification of animals from their skulls. Students will describe the characteristic features of each item and identify the species from which it came.

Traces ID practical report (10%)

This assessment item is based on the practical on the identification of animals from their traces - other than skulls and scats. Students will describe the characteristic features of each item and identify the species from which it came.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You must either hand in your assessment task in class OR submit it via the Turnitin link during the class. You should also keep an electronic copy of every piece of assessment you submit.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The quiz will test your knowledge of safety for field work, trapping and handling of wild animals, and will take place in-person at the end of the practical. This quiz is required for all students. Students are required to achieve a pass grade of at least 80% to be eligible to join the Hidden Vale trapping practical. 

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: It will take place in-person at the end of the practical.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Final examination will be based on any material covered during the course. This exam will be scheduled during the end of semester central examination period.

The exam will be an on-campus exam. Have your UQ student ID card available for all your exams.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Online tutorial series

Tutorial videos are all available online to be watched at any time; however the information will be required by the end of the week specified.

Week 1: Animal ID.

Week 3: Dichotomous Keys.

Week 4: Frog & reptile ID

Week 5: Bat Map: what bats are in my backyard?

Week 6: Traces ID.

Week 8: Trapping background information.

Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05, L06
